biodynamic.name: Weingut Peter Jakob Kuhn
biodynamic.description: Peter Jakob Kuhn farmed biodynamic from 2004 and was Demeter-certified from 2009, working the Doosberg, Lenchen, Hendelberg and Jungfer for low-intervention dry Riesling and Spaetburgunder in Oestrich.
biodynamic.tip: Compare the Doosberg and Lenchen dry Rieslings side by side; the same biodynamic farming reads two single sites a few hundred metres apart.

biodynamic.name: Weingut Prinz
biodynamic.description: Fred Prinz at Hallgarten works the Hendelberg, Jungfer and Schoenhell to biodynamic principles, certified organic from 2013, with the cooler hill-village altitude shaping a sharper, savoury dry-Riesling style.
biodynamic.tip: The Hallgartener Hendelberg GG is the wine to find; pair it with neighbour Peter Jakob Kuhn for a back-to-back biodynamic comparison.

organic.name: Weingut Baron Knyphausen
organic.description: An Erbach VDP family estate fully transitioned to organic wine production from 2021, with the historic Erbacher Marcobrunn and Kiedrich parcels in the line-up alongside classical dry Riesling and Spaetburgunder.
organic.tip: The Marcobrunn GG and Kiedrich Wasseros are the picks; visit the estate vinothek on weekday afternoons for quieter tasting.
